focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 2

The bucket hit stone at the mouth of the gallery with a flat iron note. Elise came awake at once, limestone grit at the corner of her mouth and the cold of borrowed blankets still climbing through her coat. The quarry never went wholly dark. Somewhere down the passage a lamp kept a low orange bruise on the wall, enough to turn sleeping bodies into mounds of wool and hair silvered by dust. Water dripped in the deeper chambers with patient regularity. Somebody coughed wetly two alcoves away. Far overhead, muffled by yards of stone, carriage wheels passed like distant thunder.

She counted eight sleepers in the gallery while she folded the blanket Clemence had lent her. There were nine, if the child under the table was not merely a pile of shawls.

Henri Vasseur was almost certainly in a cell by now, if he was still on his feet. Elise let that fact pass through her with the same hard flatness as the cold. Pity did not change bars into doors. It did not cancel what she had done in the street above. She tucked the blanket neatly, because untidiness was the kind of thing people remembered, and rubbed her hands over her sleeves to take the dust off.

"Good," a voice said from the mouth of the gallery. "You're up before I have to drag you."

Clemence Rigal stood with a lamp in one hand and a bucket in the other. In the bad light she still looked broad-shouldered enough to block the passage by herself. Her knuckles were roughened white from lye, the skin split in two places and healed badly. She had pinned her hair so tight it seemed to sharpen the planes of her face, but a curl had broken loose and stuck damply to her temple. Elise noticed, absurdly, that Clemence had taken the trouble to tie a faded blue ribbon around the handle of the bucket so Lucie would stop c...
